paper_metadata.title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators
paper_metadata.abstract: Masked language modeling (MLM) pre-training methods such as BERT corrupt the input by replacing some tokens with [MASK] and then train a model to reconstruct the original tokens. While they produce good results when transferred to downstream NLP tasks, they generally require large amounts of compute to be effective. As an alternative, we propose a more sample-efficient pre-training task called replaced token detection. Instead of masking the input, our approach corrupts it by replacing some tokens with plausible alternatives sampled from a small generator network. Then, instead of training a model that predicts the original identities of the corrupted tokens, we train a discriminative model that predicts whether each token in the corrupted input was replaced by a generator sample or not. Thorough experiments demonstrate this new pre-training task is more efficient than MLM because the task is defined over all input tokens rather than just the small subset that was masked out. As a result, the contextual representations learned by our approach substantially outperform the ones learned by BERT given the same model size, data, and compute. The gains are particularly strong for small models; for example, we train a model on one GPU for 4 days that outperforms GPT (trained using 30x more compute) on the GLUE natural language understanding benchmark. Our approach also works well at scale, where it performs comparably to RoBERTa and XLNet while using less than 1/4 of their compute and outperforms them when using the same amount of compute.

extraction_data.innovation: ELECTRA introduces replaced token detection (RTD), a self-supervised task where the model learns to distinguish real input tokens from high-quality negative samples generated by a small generator network. This approach is more compute-efficient than masked language modeling (MLM) and reduces the pretrain-finetune discrepancy by avoiding [MASK] tokens during pretraining.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., GLUE, SQuAD)
extraction_data.extension: Jointly trains a small masked language model (generator) to produce corrupted tokens, while the discriminator (ELECTRA) predicts whether each token was replaced. The generator is discarded after pre-training.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving pre-training efficiency and effectiveness for language understanding tasks
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering, semantic textual similarity, paraphrase detection, and natural language inference
extraction_data.paper_title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators

extraction_data.innovation: ELECTRA-Large extends the replaced token detection (RTD) pre-training task to a large-scale model, demonstrating that the efficiency gains of RTD hold at scale and outperform models like RoBERTa and XLNet with significantly less pre-training compute.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, question answering, text classification
extraction_data.research_problem: Scaling efficient pre-training methods for large language models
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., GLUE, SQuAD)
extraction_data.extension: Trained for 400K steps with a batch size of 2048, achieving comparable performance to RoBERTa with less than 1/4 of the compute.
extraction_data.paper_title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators

extraction_data.innovation: ELECTRA introduces Replaced Token Detection (RTD), a pre-training task where the model learns to distinguish real input tokens from plausible fakes generated by a small generator network. This method is more sample-efficient than Masked Language Modeling (MLM) and enables better performance with fewer pre-training FLOPs.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient pre-training of text encoders for language understanding
extraction_data.extension: Adversarial training via a generator-discriminator setup, where the generator produces corrupted tokens and the discriminator detects replacements.
extraction_data.paper_title: ELECTRA: Pre-training Text Encoders as Discriminators Rather Than Generators
